text:
Equipment: RIE

RIE Module Tutorial Content

There are 8 subsections we have identified:

Support Room Machine Startup

Front Room Machine Startup

Pre-Load Software Steps

Loading the Wafer

Etching & Running the Recipe

Removing the Wafer

Front Room Machine Shutdown

Support Room Machine Shutdown

The typical format includes 1-2 questions per section, with each having its own respective tutorial video.

DEMO VIDEO LINK: 8.1.2025 RIE Module Demo.mov

What are the specific steps for each subsection?

***There are currently 8-15 steps per subsection with a total of 87 steps.

SECTION 1: Support Room Machine Startup

Enter Support Room

“Enter the support room through the gray-colored door to get started.”

Turn On Nitrogen Tank

“Turn the nitrogen pressure valve on by rotating it counterclockwise.”

Turn Betta Mains On

“Flip the \"Mains\" switch to the on position on the Betta Industrial Heater Cooler.”

Turn Betta Cooling On

"Flip the \"Cooling\" switch to the on position on the Betta Industrial Heater Cooler.”

Start O2 Flow

“Start oxygen flow by rotating the gray knob counterclockwise.”

Turn On Oxygen Tank

Equipment: RIE

“Turn the oxygen on by rotating the pressure valve counterclockwise or to the left.”

(First) Open Etching Cabinet

“Grab the latch of orange etching gas cabinet to open."

Start Etching Gas FlowI've taken a look at the video, and I would recommend highlighting the door latch as well.

“Start SF6 flow by rotating the gray knob counterclockwise."

Turn On Etching Gas Tank

“Turn the SF6 on by rotating the pressure valve counterclockwise or to the left."

(First) Close Etching Cabinet

“Grab the latch of the orange etching gas cabinet to close."

SECTION 2: Front Room Machine Startup

Go To Front Room

“Return to the front room to continue equipment setup."

Turn On Compressed Air Valve

“Turn on the compressed air valve by turning it in-line with the piping."

Turn On Nitrogen Valve

“Turn on the nitrogen valve by turning it in-line with the piping."

Release RIE Emergency Stop

“Rotate the emergency stop slightly to the right to release."

Open RIE Door

“Open the door of the RIE machine by turning the latch vertically."

Press RIE Green On Button

“Press the green \"ON\" button to start up the RIE."

Turn On Small Chiller

“Press the power button to turn on the small chiller."

Equipment: RIE

Wait for 20 Degrees Celsius

“Wait for the small chiller to reach 20 degrees celsius."

SECTION 3: Pre-Load Software Steps

Launch PC 2000

“Go to the computer and launch PC2000 from the desktop."

Log In to Access Control

“Log in given that \ User: OPT \ Password: OPT"

Accept Opening Warnings

“Accept all initial warnings and alerts."

Press Status (Note ATP & ACT)

“Go to the RIE and press \"Status\". Note turbo pump and machine controller temperatures."

First Evacuate

“On the computer: click \"Evacuate.\""

First Load Wafer Cancel

“Click \"Cancel\" to indicate that there is no wafer in the chamber.”

Wait For Base Pressure Reached

“Go to the RIE to watch the RPM value. Wait for computer status \"Base Pressure Reached.\""

First Stop Finish Venting

“On the computer, click \"Stop.\""

First Vent

“Click \"Vent.\""

First Vent Countdown

“Wait for \"Pumping or venting stopped.\""

Second Stop Finish Venting

“Click \"Stop\" to finish venting.”

SECTION 4: Loading The Wafer

(First) Knob Chamber Up

“"Go to the RIE machine and turn the black knob to \"Chamber Up\"."

(First) Hoist Up

“Press and hold both blue \"Hoist\" buttons to gain access to the stage.")

Open Petri Dish

Equipment: RIE

“Take the lid off the petri dish used as a wafer carrier case."

Grab Wafer To Load

“Use the tweezers to remove the wafer from its case."

Place Sample Stage

“Place the wafer in the center of the RIE stage."

(First) Knob Chamber Down

“Turn the black knob to \"Chamber Down\"."

(First) Hoist Down

“Press and hold blue \"Hoist\" buttons to close the stage cover."

Second Evacuate

“On the computer, click \"Evacuate\"."

Second Load Wafer OK

“Click \"OK\" to indicate that a sample is now on the RIE stage.”

Second Wait For Base Pressure Reached

“Wait for the evacuation to complete and the status reads \"Base Pressure Reached.\"”

SECTION 5: Etching & Running the Recipe

Turn AC Line On

“Go to the RIE. Flip up the \"AC Line\" toggle switch to the on position."

Enter Process Recipe Page

“On the computer: click \"Process\" and select \"Recipes\" from the dropdown menu."

Enter Load Recipe Window

“Click \"Load\" to select a recipe."

Select Recipe, OK

“Select \"XR PRACTICE ETCH\" from the list and click \"OK.\""

Right-Click Dropdown Edit Step

“Double-click \"XR PRACTICE ETCH\" and select \"Edit Step\" from the dropdown menu."

Set Gas Values

Equipment: RIE

“Use the keyboard to set gas concentrations:\n02 = 10.0\nSF6 = 40.0"

Save Recipe Edits

“Click \"OK\" to save recipe changes."

Click Run Recipe

“Start the recipe by clicking \"Run.\""

Wait Process Step Finished

“Wait for the recipe process to finish."

Accept Yellow Alert

“Accept the yellow alert for end of process."

SECTION 6: Removing the Wafer

Return To System Pumping Page

“Click \"System\" and select \"Pumping\" from the dropdown menu."

Turn AC Line Off

“Go to the RIE. Flip down the \"AC Line\" toggle switch to the off position."

First Stop After Run

“Go to the computer and click \"Stop.\""

Vent After Run

“Click \"Vent.\" to prepare for sample retrieval."

Wait Venting Finished

“Wait until the status reads \"Venting finished.\""

Second Stop Pump Vent After Run

“Click \"Stop\" to end the venting process."

(Second) Knob Chamber Up

“Go to the RIE. Turn the black knob to \"Chamber Up\"."

(Second) Hoist Up

“Press and hold both blue \"Hoist\" buttons to gain access to the stage."

Collect Sample

“Use the tweezers to collect the sample from the stage."

Place Wafer in the Petri Dish

“Place the wafer in the petri dish."

Close Petri Lid

Equipment: RIE

“Put the lid on the petri dish to prevent contamination."

Second Knob Chamber Down

“Turn the black knob to \"Chamber Down\"."

Second Hoist Down

“Press and hold both blue \"Hoist\" buttons to close the chamber lid."

SECTION 7: Front Room Machine Shutdown

Evacuate After Run

“On the computer, click \"Evacuate\"."

(Third) Load Wafer, Cancel After Run

“Click \"Cancel\" to indicate that the wafer was removed."

Wait Base Pressure Reached After Wafer Removal

“Wait for the evacuation to complete and the status reads \"Base Pressure Reached.\""

Stop Pump Vent After Wafer Removal

“Click \"Stop\" to finish evacuating."

Enter System Service Mode

“Click \"System\" and select \"Services\" to enter Service Mode."

Click Pump Graphic

“Click the pump part of the RIE graphic."

Yes Turn Pump Off

“Select \"Yes\" to disable pump activity."

Wait Turbo Speed 0 RPM

“Watch the RIE screen for the turbo pump to reach 0 rpm."

System Exit Service, OK

“On the computer: click \"System\" and select \"Exit Services\" from the dropdown. Click \"OK\"."

System Exit Application, Yes

“Click \"System\" and select \"Exit\" from the dropdown. Click \"Yes\" to confirm"

Press RIE Red Off Button

Equipment: RIE

“Go to the RIE machine and press the red \"Off\" button."

Close RIE Door

“Turn the latch of the RIE machine door horizontally to close it."

Press (Reactivate) Emergency Stop

“Press the E-Stop inwards to re-activate it."

Turn Off Compressed Air Valve

“Turn off the compressed air valve by rotating it 90 degrees back to its original position."

Turn Off Nitrogen Valve

“Turn off the nitrogen valve by rotating it 90 degrees back to its original position."

SECTION 8: Support Room Machine Shutdown

Return Support Room

“Return to the support room to continue shutoff procedures."

(Second) Open Etching Cabinet

“Grab the latch of the orange etching gas cabinet to open."

Turn Off Etching Tank

“Turn the SF6 off by rotating the pressure valve clockwise or to the right."

Stop Etching Tank Flow

“Stop SF6 flow by rotating the gray knob clockwise."

Second Close Etching Cabinet

“Grab the latch of the orange etching gas cabinet to close."

Turn Off Oxygen Tank

“Turn the oxygen off by rotating the pressure valve clockwise or to the right."

Stop Oxygen Flow

“Stop oxygen flow by rotating the gray knob clockwise."

Turn Betta Cooling Off

Equipment: RIE

“Flip the \"Cooling\" switch to the off position on the Betta Industrial Heater Cooler."

Turn Betta Mains Off

“Flip the \"Mains\" switch to the off position on the Betta Industrial Heater Cooler."

Turn Off Nitrogen Tank

“Turn the nitrogen pressure valve off by rotating it clockwise."

RIE Quiz Questions

1) Support Room Machine Startup Question Ideas

T/F: When turning on the oxygen tank, you first rotate the gray flow knob on the top of the tank, then the pressure valve.

A: True

T/F: To turn on the gas tanks you rotate the valve clockwise.

A: False. The statement is backwards. You rotate the valves counterclockwise.

2) Front Room Machine Startup Question Ideas

Q: What temperature should the front-room chiller reach before you start using the computer to run the RIE machine? A. 20C B. 21C C. 22C D.23C E.24C

A: A. 20C

3) Pre-Load Software Steps

Q: When reading the screen of the RIE, what does ATP and ACT refer to?

A. ATP: Turbo Pump Temperature, ACT: Machine Controller Temperature

T/F: You should monitor ATP ACT values at all times.

A: True

Q: What is the purpose of evacuation?

A. To introduce reactive gases for etching.

Equipment: RIE

B. To remove unwanted gases and contaminants.

C. To cool down the wafer during the etching process.

D. To increase the etch rate and anisotropy.

E. To enable high-pressure plasma generation.

A: B, evacuation removes unwanted gases and creates a low-pressure environment for plasma generation.

4) Loading the Wafer

Q: Select the correct order of steps to load the wafer on the stage.

A. Open the petri dish > Grab the wafer with gloved hands > Place it on the stage.

B. Open the petri dish > Take it to the stage > Tip the wafer out of the dish onto the stage.

C. Open the petri dish > Grab the wafer using a pair of tweezers > Place it on the stage.

A: C

Q: How do you open the chamber lid?

A. Press one of the hoist buttons.

B. Turn the switch to chamber up, and it will open automatically.

C. Turn the switch to chamber up and press one of the hoist buttons.

D. Turn the switch to chamber up, then press and hold both of the hoist buttons.

E. Turn the switch to chamber up and press both of the hoist buttons.

A: D

5) Etching and Running the Recipe

T/F: You should never change the gas concentrations when running a recipe.

Equipment: RIE

A: False, you should always check the flow rate, process time, and power values before running a recipe and adjust them if needed.

Q: Why is plasma necessary during Reactive Ion Etching? Plasma...

A. Acts as a heat source to melt and remove the material.

B. Provides UV radiation to solidify the patterned mask.

C. Generates reactive ions and radicals that chemically react with the material and physically sputter it away.

D. Creates a vacuum that draws material away from the substrate.

E. Forms a protective layer to prevent etching of the substrate.

A: C Ions bombard the surface, physically removing the material and enhancing the vertical etch directionality (anisotropy).

6) Removing the Wafer

T/F: It is impossible to see a difference in the sample’s appearance after etching without the use of a microscope.

A: False

T/F: You must wait for the turbo pump to reach 0 rpm before exiting service mode on the computer.

A: True.

T/F: When turning the AC line on or off, you should flip the switch quickly.

A: False

7) Front Room Machine Shutdown

T/F: The small chiller automatically turns off when you press the red power “OFF” button on the RIE machine.

A: True.

Equipment: RIE

Q: Which of the following is the correct order of actions to evacuate the chamber after running a recipe?

A. Click Evacuate > Click Popup OK > Wait For Base Pressure Reached > Click Stop

B. Click Evacuate > Click Popup Cancel > Click Stop

C. Click Evacuate > Click Popup OK > Click Stop

D. Click Evacuate > Click Popup Cancel > Wait For Base Pressure Reached > Click Stop

A: D (Evacuate, Cancel, Wait Base Pressure Reached, Stop)

8) Support Room Machine Shutdown

T/F: When shutting down the support room machines, you turn them off in the exact same order you turned them on.

A: False, you turn them off in the exact opposite direction.

T/F: In commercial settings, it is likely that gases will remain on all day.

A: True
